data.title: France finds Wish, Temu, AliExpress, eBay, Joom, Amazon broke rules on illicit products
data.description:
A French consumer watchdog found that besides Chinese fast-fashion retailer Shein, five other online platforms sold illicit products in France, a spokesperson for Commerce Minister Serge Papin said on Friday.

The watchdog, called DGCCRF, said AliExpress and Joom were selling child-like sex dolls, while weapons such as brass knuckles and machetes were available on Wish, Temu and eBay, the spokesperson said, confirming an article by Le Parisien newspaper.

DGCCRF found that Wish, Temu and U.S.-based Amazon , the largest e-commerce company by market value, failed to filter underage shoppers from adult content, he said.

The spokesperson said Papin referred the platforms to public prosecutors and would invite the European Union's commerce ministers to Paris to discuss this issue.

Amazon, Temu, eBay, AliExpress and Joom did not immediately respond to emails seeking comment.

The broad French crackdown on large foreign e-commerce leaders is an attempt to protect local retailers who feel threatened by unfair competition from cheaper and better-marketed products on their own turf.

Papin used to lead supermarket chain Super U.

Similar EU initiatives include the decision on Thursday to bring forward by one year the customs duties on low-value parcels arriving in the bloc to crack down on cheap Chinese e-commerce imports.

Earlier this month, the French consumer watchdog sparked outrage against Shein when it said it had spotted child-like sex dolls, weapons such as machetes and knives, and other illicit products on the Chinese-owned marketplace.

A public prosecutor then opened an investigation into Shein as well as Temu, AliExpress and Wish for different potential breaches of rules.

Following the furore over Shein's marketplace, the French government started the process to block the platform in the country, with Finance Minister Roland Lescure threatening to entirely suspend the website in France if banned products continue to be found for sale.

A week ago, after acknowledging Shein had withdrawn all illicit products from its website, the government suspended the process to block Shein and said it was looking into other platforms it did not identify.

data.title: PLATTS: 100--US POWER TRACKER: PJM power forwards higher with spot gas up 34%
data.description: (Platts)--14Nov2025/341 pm EST/2041 GMT **NI Hub power forwards up 103% **TX Eastern gas forwards reach $8.98/MMBtu in Jan PJM Interconnection forward power prices are up considerably from year-ago spot levels with spot natural gas prices trading 34% higher year on year in October. Spot power and gas prices have risen during the first cold snap of the season. Prompt-month Northern Illinois Hub on-peak forward prices are about 103% higher than spot prices at the hub in October 2024. Power forwards averaged $56.70/MWh in recent trading compared to $27.89/MWh a year ago. PJM West Hub on-peak forward prices for December recently traded at $75.76, up almost 89% from year-ago spot price levels. And AEP-Dayton Hub on-peak forwards traded at $65.75/MWh, up nearly 79% from year-ago spot price levels. Texas Eastern M-3 forward gas prices for December delivery recently traded at $6.40/MMBtu, up from an October 2024 average of $1.59/MMBtu, according to Platts data. Platts is part of S&P Global Commodity Insights. Dominion Hub on-peak forward power prices peak at $98.90/MWh for January 2026 delivery, the highest level in the three-month forward strip. Texas Eastern M-3 gas forwards peak at $8.98/MMBtu for January 2026 delivery. SPOT POWER, GAS PRICE DYNAMICS Northern Illinois Hub on-peak day-ahead power prices averaged $41.18/MWh in October, up about 35% year on year and up almost 5% month on month. Month-to-date prices at the hub have averaged $44.46/MWh with a daily average of $60.12/MWh on Nov. 10 when temperatures across the PJM footprint averaged 39.6 degrees Fahrenheit, according to CustomWeather data. PJM West Hub on-peak day-ahead power prices averaged $56.79/MWh in October, up about 42% year on year and up about 17% month on month. Prices at the hub have averaged $59.84/MWh thus far in November, with a daily average peak of $91.39/MWh on Nov. 11 when temperatures averaged 36.4 F. Texas Eastern gas prices averaged $2.13/MMBtu in October, up 34% year on year and up about 22% month on month. Prices have averaged $3.06/MMBtu thus far in November with a daily average peak of $3.27/MMBtu on Nov. 11 and 12. PJM peakload averaged 92,290 MW in October, up from a September average of 106,811 MW when higher temperatures pushed up electric cooling demand. The average temperature across the PJM footprint in September was 69.9 F compared with an October average of 58.4 F. Cooling degree days averaged 4.9 in September compared with an average of 0.8 in October. INTERCONNECTION QUEUE The PJM power grid interconnection queue is dominated by projects with expected commercial operational dates in December. The projects included in the data include "Active" projects meaning they are undergoing studies with PJM. Projects under engineering and procurement are included as well as those under construction. Overall, the queue is dominated by solar power projects, battery storage projects and projects that pair solar power with batteries. Projects in the queue do not all get built, but the data provides a view into the types of resources that developers are pursuing. Some larger gas-fired projects appear in 2026, with an 850-MW project with a projected commercial operational date of January and a 2,075-MW project listed as expected online in July. In early November, Constellation Energy released a proposal of fast-track projects to address Maryland near-term needs in response to a solicitation issued by the state, according to the latest S&P Global North American Electricity Short-term Outlook. "The proposal includes 714 MW of new gas-fired peaker capacity in addition to 800 MW of 4-hour batteries," S&P Global analysts said. "These projects fall under the umbrella of a longer-term proposal made by Constellation, which includes up to 5.8 GW of new generation and storage." — Jared Anderson, jared.anderson@spglobal.com-- Edited by Richard Rubin, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

Fitch: Brazilian Central Bank VASPs Regulation Is Positive for Market Development
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Rio de Janeiro-14 November 2025: Brazil’s Central Bank (Bacen) resolutions on virtual asset service providers (VASPs) firms that offer cryptocurrency exchange, custody, and related digital asset services mark a significant regulatory step that will strengthen market practices while imposing higher compliance costs that may consolidate the sector, Fitch Ratings says. The framework, effective February 2026, aligns Brazil with global standards and reduces operational risks, though smaller firms face challenges from new capital and governance requirements.

Resolutions 519, 520 and 521, published on Nov. 10, 2025, establish comprehensive licensing, prudential requirements and activity-based limitations for VASPs and authorized institutions. The regulations formalize custody segregation of client crypto assets, introduce minimum corporate governance standards requiring at least three directors for key functions, and prohibit combined intermediary-custodian activities to limit conflicts of interest. Banks, securities brokers and FX brokers can offer digital asset and cryptocurrency services upon notification and certification. Required operational safeguards recognize Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) concepts including hot, warm and cold wallets, smart contracts, stablecoins, and proof of reserves.

Fitch expects the framework to support market integrity and reduce operational and legal risks, while imposing compliance and certification costs related to technology and cybersecurity that may limit smaller, thinly capitalized firms. Fitch also expects the regulation to strengthen market practices, operational resilience and transparency. New certifications and minimum corporate governance will increase costs but will have a positive impact by increasing robustness and reducing the potential for operational losses and legal risks.

The framework's alignment with anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism (AML/CFT) enforcement and global tokenization standards supports Brazil's position as a regional digital finance hub while maintaining financial system stability. Restrictions on credit and deposit-taking—limiting VASP borrowing to twice regulatory capital—aim to contain risk outside Bacen's desired scope for traditional financial institutions.

The 270-day transition period for existing institutions requires compliance with governance, product and operational provisions. Fitch expects larger banks and established brokers eligible to offer custody and intermediation of digital assets to benefit from clearer rules, reputational advantages and economies of scale in controls, potentially gaining market share from smaller VASPs. Smaller and foreign VASPs face challenges as the transition will translate into higher costs from specialized technical certification and corporate governance investments which could drive market consolidation.

data.title: BTCY: Revenue up 20% to $7.8M, gross margin 81.2%, EBITDA positive, but going concern risk persists
data.description:
Revenue grew 20% year-over-year to $7.8M with gross margin rising to 81.2%, and EBITDA turned positive for the second consecutive quarter. Net loss narrowed to $1.36M, and free cash flow was positive, but substantial doubt remains about the ability to continue as a going concern.

Original document: Biotricity Inc. [BTCY] SEC 10-Q Quarterly Report — Nov. 14 2025

data.title: FAMILY OFFICE OF AMERICA, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Family Office of America, Inc., an early-stage company focused on acquiring interests in CPA firms and providing comprehensive family office services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and strategic business initiatives aimed at expanding its footprint in the CPA and family office services industry.

Financial Highlights

Net Revenues: The company reported no revenues for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, and 2024.

Operating Expenses: For the three months ended September 30, 2025, operating expenses were $55,169, a 157.3% increase from $21,438 for the same period in 2024. For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, operating expenses were $257,955, a 282.3% increase from $67,473 for the same period in 2024. The increase was primarily due to higher consulting fees, travel costs, warrants for services, and stock-based compensation.

Net Loss Before Income Taxes: The company reported a net loss before income taxes of $(50,944) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(22,676) for the same period in 2024. For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, the net loss before income taxes was $(249,493), compared to $(70,064) for the same period in 2024. The increased net loss was primarily due to higher operating expenses.

Other (Income) Expense: For the three months ended September 30, 2025, other income was $(4,225), primarily due to interest income, compared to $1,238 in interest expense for the same period in 2024. For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, other income was $(8,462), primarily due to interest income, compared to $2,591 in interest expense for the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

Business Focus: Family Office of America, Inc. is dedicated to acquiring interests in CPA firms and providing a suite of family office services, including financial planning, investment management, and tax services. The company aims to offer a comprehensive solution for managing the financial and investment needs of affluent individuals and families.

Industry Context: The CPA industry, valued at $147.5 billion in 2023, presents significant opportunities for consolidation and automation, especially as a large portion of CPAs approach retirement age. Family Office of America plans to leverage this trend by providing succession plans and growth opportunities for CPA firms.

Service Offerings: The company offers a wide range of services, including CPA services, tax planning and preparation, wealth management, asset management, estate planning, asset protection, insurance consulting, and investment banking.

Strategic Expansion: Family Office of America plans to expand its operations by opening new offices in several cities, including Phoenix, AZ; Centennial, CO; Houston, TX; Nashville, TN; Charlotte, NC; Orlando, FL; Ft. Lauderdale, FL; and Cocoa Beach, FL.

Acquisition Strategy: The company intends to purchase minority or full interests in CPA practices, offering a significant portion in cash. This strategy aims to integrate smaller firms into the Family Office of America platform, providing them with platform services and an exit strategy.

Asset Purchase Agreement: On October 1, 2025, Family Office of Maryland, LLC, a subsidiary of the company, acquired certain assets from Toone & Associates, LLP, related to non-attest services. This acquisition includes client lists, tangible personal property, intellectual property, accounts receivable, and goodwill associated with accounting services.

Consultant Engagement: The company has retained Bruce Toone as a consultant and manager for two tax seasons to provide public accounting services and assist in introducing Family Office Services to clients.

Operational Challenges: The company faces challenges due to its limited operating history and the need for additional capital to support daily operations and future growth. Management is focused on expanding operations and increasing revenues to address these challenges.

Future Outlook: Management is optimistic about the company's strategy to generate revenues and raise additional funds through public offerings or asset sales. However, there are no assurances that these efforts will be successful or on terms favorable to the company.

SEC Filing: FAMILY OFFICE OF AMERICA, INC. [ FOFA ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Eco Bright Future, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Eco Bright Future, Inc., a company focused on providing digital assets from El Salvador, tokenizing assets, and developing blockchain tools, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, shedding light on its ongoing challenges and future plans.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: $0. The company did not recognize any revenue during the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $1,446 in the same period in 2024.

Operating Loss: $(32,475). Operating expenses increased slightly to $32,475 in 2025 from $31,507 in 2024, primarily due to increased general and administrative expenses.

Net Income (Loss): $(32,507). The net loss for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was slightly higher than the net loss of $29,934 in the same period in 2024.

Basic and Diluted Loss Per Common Share: $(0.00). The loss per share remained unchanged from the previous year, reflecting the company's ongoing financial challenges.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: During the reporting period, Eco Bright Future, Inc. did not recognize any revenue from its intended operations, which include providing digital assets from El Salvador, tokenizing assets, and developing blockchain tools. The company had previously recognized $1,446 in revenue from non-recurring consulting services related to blockchain technology software development sales in the same period last year.

Geographical Performance: The company operates in foreign countries, specifically Tunisia, and plans to expand its blockchain products to markets in Thailand, Indonesia, and Guatemala. However, no specific revenue or performance data from these regions was reported for the current period.

New Product Launches: The company is focused on creating a virtual investment vehicle on the blockchain linked to tangible assets such as real estate, precious metals, art, and collectibles.

Future Outlook: Eco Bright Future, Inc. plans to continue its expansion efforts by entering into agreements for its blockchain products in new markets, including Thailand, Indonesia, and Guatemala. The company anticipates that losses from operations will increase in the next twelve months due to expected increases in payroll expenses and legal and accounting costs associated with maintaining a reporting company.

Operational Challenges: The company faces substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to accumulated losses and negative cash flows from operations. Management plans to address these challenges by generating and growing revenue and accessing capital markets to achieve strategic objectives.

SEC Filing: Eco Bright Future, Inc. [ EBFI ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: BIOTRICITY INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Biotricity Inc., a leading provider of remote monitoring solutions for preventative care, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing notable improvements in both financial performance and business operations. The report highlights the company's strides in revenue growth, cost management, and product commercialization efforts, despite ongoing challenges related to working capital and accumulated deficits.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $7.76 million, reflecting an increase from $6.47 million in the prior year period, driven by higher technology fees and device sales.
  • Gross Profit: $6.30 million, up from $4.82 million, indicating improved cost management and increased sales volume.
  • Profit (Loss) from Operations: $0.56 million, compared to a loss of $1.42 million in the previous year, highlighting a significant turnaround in operational efficiency.
  • Net Loss Before Income Taxes: $(1.36) million, an improvement from a loss of $(5.26) million, due to better cost control and increased revenue.
  • Net Loss Attributable to Common Stockholders: $(1.53) million, compared to $(8.60) million, showing a substantial reduction in losses.
  • Loss Per Share, Basic and Diluted: $(0.057), improved from $(0.469), reflecting the company's efforts in reducing losses per share.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue from two main segments: technology fees and device sales. Technology fees accounted for a significant portion of the revenue, with $6,882,846 for the six months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $6,081,064 for the same period in 2024. Device sales also increased, reaching $876,942 for the six months ended September 30, 2025, up from $387,525 in the prior year.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates primarily in the United States, with its property and equipment and operating right of use lease assets located there. The Canadian-based subsidiary's functional currency is the Canadian dollar, indicating some level of international operations.
  • New Product Launches: The company is focused on the commercialization of its Bioflux, Biocore, and Biocare product lines, indicating ongoing efforts to expand its product offerings in the remote monitoring segment of preventative care.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued revenue growth and improved liquidity through business development and additional equity and debt capitalization. The company plans to devote significant resources to capital expenditures, research and development, and marketing and sales as it proceeds with the commercialization of its product lines.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faces challenges related to its working capital deficiency and accumulated deficit, which raise substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern. Management is focused on addressing these challenges through revenue growth and financing activities.

SEC Filing: BIOTRICITY INC. [ BTCY ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Ark 21Shares Bitcoin ETF SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Ark 21Shares Bitcoin ETF, a Delaware statutory trust designed to track the performance of bitcoin, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report reveals substantial financial gains driven by bitcoin price appreciation and provides insights into the Trust's operational strategies and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Net realized and change in unrealized gain on investment in bitcoin: $320,440 thousand, driven by bitcoin price appreciation throughout the period.

Net increase in net assets resulting from operations:

  • $317,667 thousand for the three months ended September 30, 2025, primarily driven by net realized and change in unrealized gain on investment in bitcoin.
  • $927,779 thousand for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, resulting from a change in unrealized depreciation on investment in bitcoin and net realized gains on investment in bitcoin sold for redemptions.
  • $46,097 thousand for the three months ended September 30, 2024, largely due to realized gains on the sale of bitcoin for Sponsor’s fee and redemptions.
  • $481,493 thousand for the nine months ended September 30, 2024, driven by a change in unrealized depreciation on investment in bitcoin and net realized gains on investment in bitcoin sold for redemptions.

Business Highlights

Trust Overview: The ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF is a Delaware statutory trust formed to track the performance of bitcoin, as measured by the CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate—New York Variant. The Trust is an exchange-traded fund that issues common units of beneficial interest, trading on the Cboe BZX Exchange under the ticker symbol 'ARKB'.

Investment Strategy: The Trust's investment strategy is concentrated solely in bitcoin, maximizing exposure to market risks associated with digital assets. The Trust does not actively manage the bitcoin held and is a passive investment vehicle.

Share Split: On June 2, 2025, the Trust announced a 3-for-1 share split, effective June 16, 2025. This split did not change the total net asset value of the Trust or the percentage of shares held by each Record Holder.

Sponsor Fee: The Trust pays a unitary Sponsor fee of 0.21% of its bitcoin holdings. The Sponsor agreed to waive this fee for the first nine months after the Trust's shares were listed or until the Trust's assets exceeded $1 billion, which occurred in February 2024.

Operational Expenses: The Sponsor covers all ordinary operating expenses, including fees for the Sub-Adviser, Custodians, and other service providers, except for extraordinary expenses such as legal claims or litigation costs.

Creation and Redemption of Shares: Shares are created and redeemed in Creation Baskets of 5,000 shares or multiples thereof, with transactions conducted in cash. Only Authorized Participants can place orders for these transactions.

Future Outlook: The Trust aims to continue tracking the performance of bitcoin, with no changes to its investment objective, strategy, or underlying holdings anticipated. The Trust remains focused on maintaining its position as a passive investment vehicle.

SEC Filing: Ark 21Shares Bitcoin ETF [ ARKB ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Barrick Mining rises on report co mulling splitting into two entities
data.description:
** Shares of Canadian gold miner Barrick Mining rise 2.1% to C$52.41

** Reuters reports, citing four sources aware of the development, ABX is considering the possibility of splitting the company into two separate entities

** One entity would be focused on North America and the other centered on Africa and Asia, report adds

** Split could also include the outright sale of Barrick's African assets and the Reko Diq mine in Pakistan after the company secures financing for Reko Diq - report

** ABX is also looking to resolve dispute with Mali's military administration before selling the asset - report

** Including session's moves, stock has jumped ~37% YTD

data.title: RDI: Q3 2025 delivered improved profitability and lower debt, with optimism for a strong Q4 rebound
data.description:
Q3 2025 saw revenues decline 13% year-over-year, but net loss improved 41% and EBITDA rose 26%, marking five straight quarters of positive EBITDA. Debt was reduced by nearly 15% through major property sales, and management anticipates a strong Q4 and 2026 driven by a robust film slate.

Original document: Reading International Inc [RDI] SEC 8-K Current Report — Nov. 18 2025

data.title: Under Armour and Steph Curry are breaking up. The move is partly about 'discipline,' CEO says.
data.description:
By Bill Peters

'For Stephen, it's the right moment to let what we created evolve on his terms,' CEO says

The Under Armour sneakers worn by Stephen Curry of the Golden State Warriors.

After more than a decade together, Under Armour Inc. and NBA star Stephen Curry on Thursday said they would be parting ways, as the athleisure giant moves ahead with a turnaround effort and a deeper focus on its core products.

Under Armour (UA), which inked a deal in 2013 to put Curry in its basketball shoes, made the announcement on the same day it announced an "expansion" of an earlier restructuring plan that included the separation of the Curry brand, as well as other contract terminations and "additional employee severance and benefits costs."

Under Armour said it doesn't expect the separation from Curry to significantly affect its financial results or profits. Shares were down around 3% on Friday.

"For Under Armour, this moment is about discipline and focus on the core UA brand during a critical stage of our turnaround," founder and Chief Executive Kevin Plank said in a statement. "And for Stephen, it's the right moment to let what we created evolve on his terms."

Under the terms of the breakup, Curry's brand will become independent. But the company will still release the Curry 13, the final Curry brand shoe with Under Armour, in February, with other variations and clothing available through next October.

Under Armour has been dealing with stiffer competition, tariffs and a sagging stock price, as consumers shy away from spending heavily on shoes and clothing while trying to cover other basics. The company said it would focus on its namesake brand and develop new basketball products on its own.

"Under Armour believed in me early in my career and gave me the space to build something much bigger and more impactful than a shoe," Curry, who plays for the Golden State Warriors and is widely viewed as the best three-point shooter in NBA history, said in a statement. "I'll always be grateful for that."

The company on Thursday also said its board had approved an extra $95 million in restructuring actions, bringing the total estimated charges to as much as $255 million. Around $34 million of that would go toward employee severance and benefits costs.

A representative for the company said the 2025 restructuring plan covers two fiscal years - 2025 and 2026 - and that the $34 million was the full estimate for severance and benefits for both years. Most of those costs are connected with actions the company has already taken.

The representative added that the plan should be largely complete by the end of fiscal 2026, which runs through March.

"Therefore, we do not anticipate significant additional workforce reductions beyond those already executed and included in the plan," the representative said.

Shares of Under Armour are down more than 40% so far this year. Plank returned to the company as CEO last year, but analysts said the move reflected continued uncertainty. Sales fell in Under Armour's fiscal second quarter, the results of which were reported last week.

Under Armour has tried to narrow down its product selection, sell at higher prices and strengthen marketing efforts, William Blair analyst Dylan Carden said in a note last week. But he said he felt the company was still in the early stages of that process.

"While valuation is not demanding, we view the lack of clear visibility into a meaningful inflection point as the primary overhang on the stock," he added.

However, the Curry brand had been characterized as a positive on recent earnings calls.

"The Curry brand continues to expand its impact with the steady flow of the new Curry 12 and the De'Aaron Fox 1 colorways, along with exclusive athlete designs, keeping the brand in view and culturally relevant," Plank said on Under Armour's earnings call in May.

-Bill Peters

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Activist investor Irenic builds stake in Papa John's, sources say
data.description:
Key points:
  • Unclear what Irenic might want to push for
  • Size of hedge fund's stake not known
  • Irenic appears at time of uncertainty for Papa John's

By Svea Herbst-Bayliss and Abigail Summerville

Activist investor Irenic Capital Management built a stake in Papa John's during the third quarter, sources familiar with the matter told Reuters on Friday, adding a fresh dimension to mounting speculation about the pizza chain's future.

Generally, when an activist hedge fund builds a new holding, it signals interest in forcing management to consider alternatives. But it was not immediately clear what changes, if any, Irenic will seek at Papa John's.

A representative for Irenic declined to comment, while a representative for Papa John's did not respond to a request for comment. The size of Irenic's stake was not known.

The stake could be made public when Irenic makes its 13F filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, which requires fund managers to detail their ownership in publicly traded U.S. companies for a given three-month period.

Potential buyers have been circling the pizza chain for months but may have been frightened off as U.S. consumers are pulling back on spending at quick-serve restaurant chains like Papa John's.

Apollo Global recently withdrew an offer to take the chain private at $64 a share, Reuters reported earlier this month, helping send the company's stock tumbling.

With no offer on the table, Papa John's CEO Todd Penegor told analysts on last week's quarterly earnings call that while the board would certainly look at all proposals, it was now moving ahead on executing its own strategy.

Industry analysts and investors said they supported Penegor's candid and pragmatic assessment, helping the stock price recover some.

Its shares seesawed this week after a website called ABC Money published a report on Monday saying there was a new bid for the company, driving its stock north. Its shares tumbled on Tuesday after other media outlets dismissed the story as a hoax. A source close to Papa John's confirmed that the report was false.

Some investors said Papa John's remains a desirable property but given new economic uncertainties, it might take time for potential bidders to come to an appropriate price.

Irenic was founded by former Elliott Investment Management executive Adam Katz and former Indaba Capital Management executive Andy Dodge and has been involved when companies like Arconic, Barnes and Couchbase went private and mounted activist engagements at News Corp and Theravance Biopharma .

data.title: Sarepta's Elevidys to Get Updated Labeling, Indication
data.description:
By Terell Wright

Sarepta Therapeutics said it received approval from the Food & Drug Administration to update prescribing information for Elevidys, its gene therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

The genetic engineering biotechnology company said Friday that the updated Elevidys label would now carry a boxed warning regarding the risk of acute serious liver injury and liver failure, as well as a new warnings and precautions label about increased susceptibility to serious infections due to immunosuppression.

The indication for treating non-ambulatory patients has been removed from the drug's approved uses.

Prescribing guidance was also expanded to include a modified corticosteroid regimen and enhanced weekly patient monitoring.

Sarepta said it plans to quickly start a new study using an enhanced sirolimus immunosuppressive regimen to seek authorization to treat non-ambulatory patients again.

Write to Terell Wright at terell.wright@wsj.com

Fitch Assigns Expected Ratings to HOMES 2025-AFC4; Presale Issued
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-New York-14 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings expects to rate the residential mortgage-backed certificates to be issued by HOMES 2025-AFC4 Trust (HOMES 2025-AFC4).

HOMES 2025-AFC4

----A1A ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; AAA(EXP)sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----A1B ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; AAA(EXP)sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----A1 ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; AAA(EXP)sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----A2 ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----A3 ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----M1 ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----B1 ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----B2 ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----B3 ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----AIOS ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----XS ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----R ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

Transaction Summary

The certificates are supported by 1,098 nonprime loans with a total balance of approximately $461.5 million as of the cutoff date.

Loans in the pool were originated or purchased by AmWest Funding Corporation (AmWest) and are currently serviced by AmWest. HOMES 2025-AFC4 has a weighted average Fitch (WA) FICO of 753 and a mark-to-market (MtM) combined loan-to-value (cLTV) of 66.6%. The pool consists of 56.6% of loans secured by a borrower's primary residence, while 43.4% consist of second-home or investor properties. Of the pool, 99.6% was underwritten with less than full documentation. Specifically, 38.6% of the pool was underwritten to a profit and loss (P&L) statement, 27.0% was a debt-service coverage ratio (DSCR) product and 1.5% was a no-ratio product, 16.3% was underwritten to a written verification of employment (WVOE) program, and 16.1% was underwritten to a bank statement program. Of the pool, 54.6% are non-qualified mortgages (NQM).

Distributions of principal and interest (P&I) and loss allocations are based on a modified sequential-payment structure with limited advance.

KEY RATING DRIVERS

Credit Risk of Mortgage Assets: RMBS transactions are directly affected by the performance of the underlying residential mortgages or mortgage-related assets. Fitch analyzes loan-level attributes and macroeconomic factors to assess the credit risk and expected losses. HOMES 2025-AFC4 has a final probability of default (PD) of 45.0% in the 'AAAsf' rating stress. Fitch's final loss severity in the 'AAAsf' rating stress is 38.5%. The expected loss in the 'AAAsf' rating stress is 17.3%.

Structural Analysis: The mortgage cash flow and loss allocation in HOMES 2025-AFC4 are based on a modified sequential-payment structure, whereby principal is distributed pro rata to the senior notes while excluding the subordinate bonds from principal distributions until all senior classes are reduced to zero. If a cumulative loss trigger event or a delinquency trigger event occurs in a given period, principal will be distributed sequentially to the class A-1A, A-1B, A-2 and A-3 notes until they are reduced to zero.

Fitch analyzes the capital structure to determine the adequacy of the transaction's credit enhancement (CE) to support payments on the securities under multiple scenarios incorporating Fitch's loss projections derived from the asset analysis. Fitch applies its assumptions for defaults, prepayments, delinquencies and interest rate scenarios. The CE for all ratings was sufficient at the given rating levels.

Operational Risk Analysis: Fitch considers originator and servicer capability, third-party due diligence results, and the transaction-specific representation, warranty and enforcement (RW&E) framework to derive a potential operational risk adjustment. The only consideration that has a direct impact on Fitch's loss expectations is third-party due diligence. Third-party due diligence was performed on all loans in the transaction. Fitch applies a 5bps z-score reduction for loans fully reviewed by a third-party review (TPR) firm, that have a final grade of either 'A' or 'B'.

Counterparty and Legal Analysis: Fitch expects all relevant transaction parties to comply with the requirements set out in its "Global Structured Finance Rating Criteria". Relevant parties are those whose failure to perform could have a material impact on transaction performance. Additionally, all legal requirements should be satisfied to fully delink the transaction from any other entities. Fitch expects HOMES 2025-AFC4 to be fully delinked and to serve as a bankruptcy-remote special-purpose vehicle (SPV). All transaction parties and triggers align with Fitch's expectations.

Rating Cap Analysis: Common rating caps in U.S. RMBS may include, but are not limited to, new product types with limited or volatile historical data and transactions with weak operational or structural/counterparty features. These considerations do not apply to HOMES 2025-AFC4; as such, Fitch is comfortable assigning the highest possible rating of 'AAAsf' without any rating caps.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

Fitch incorporates a sensitivity analysis to demonstrate how the ratings would react to steeper market value declines (MVDs) than assumed at the MSA level. Sensitivity analysis was conducted at the state and national level to assess the effect of higher MVDs for the subject pool as well as lower MVDs, illustrated by a gain in home prices.

The defined negative rating sensitivity analysis demonstrates how the ratings would react to steeper MVDs at the national level. The analysis assumes MVDs of 10.0%, 20.0% and 30.0% in addition to the model projected 36.9% at 'AAA'. The analysis indicates that there is some potential rating migration with higher MVDs for all rated classes, compared with the model projection. A 10% additional decline in home prices would lower all rated classes by one full category.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

The defined positive rating sensitivity analysis demonstrates how the ratings would react to positive home price growth of 10% with no assumed overvaluation. Excluding the senior class, which is already rated 'AAAsf', the analysis indicates there is potential positive rating migration for all of the rated classes. Specifically, a 10% gain in home prices would result in a full category upgrade for the rated class excluding those being assigned ratings of 'AAAsf'.

USE OF THIRD PARTY DUE DILIGENCE PURSUANT TO SEC RULE 17G -10

Fitch was provided with Form ABS Due Diligence-15E (Form 15E) as prepared by Canopy, Clarifii and Clayton. The third-party due diligence described in Form 15E focused on credit, compliance, and property valuation review. Fitch considered this information in its analysis and, as a result, Fitch made the following adjustment to its analysis: a 5% credit at the loan level for each loan where satisfactory due diligence was completed.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES AND ENFORCEMENT MECHANISMS A description of the transaction's representations, warranties and enforcement mechanisms (RW&Es) that are disclosed in the offering document and which relate to the underlying asset pool is available by clicking the link to the Appendix. The appendix also contains a comparison of these RW&Es to those Fitch considers typical for the asset class as detailed in the Special Report titled 'Representations, Warranties and Enforcement Mechanisms in Global Structured Finance Transactions'.

ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

Applicable Model

PLEASE READ THESE LIMITATIONS AND DISCLAIMERS BY FOLLOWING THIS LINK: HTTPS://WWW.FITCHRATINGS.COM/UNDERSTANDINGCREDITRATINGS. IN ADDITION, THE FOLLOWING HTTPS://WWW.FITCHRATINGS.COM/RATING-DEFINITIONS-DOCUMENT DETAILS FITCH'S RATING DEFINITIONS FOR EACH RATING SCALE AND RATING CATEGORIES, INCLUDING DEFINITIONS RELATING TO DEFAULT. PUBLISHED RATINGS, CRITERIA, AND METHODOLOGIES ARE AVAILABLE FROM THIS SITE AT ALL TIMES. FITCH'S CODE OF CONDUCT, CONFIDENTIALITY, CONFLICTS OF INTEREST, AFFILIATE FIREWALL, COMPLIANCE, AND OTHER RELEVANT POLICIES AND PROCEDURES ARE ALSO AVAILABLE FROM THE CODE OF CONDUCT SECTION OF THIS SITE. data.title: Alibaba Denies Report It Helped China Identify Military Targets in U.S.
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Alibaba Group denied a report that said it provides tech support for the Chinese military to target the U.S.

"The assertions and innuendos in the article are completely false," Alibaba said in a statement.

The Financial Times reported Friday that it viewed a White House national security memo that includes declassified intelligence on how Alibaba supplies the People's Liberation Army with capabilities that could threaten U.S. security.

The Financial Times said in its report that it couldn't independently verify the claims.

Alibaba provides the Chinese government and PLA with access to customer data including IP addresses, WiFi information and payment records, the Financial Times reported, citing the memo.

"We question the motivation behind the anonymous leak," Alibaba said in its statement.

American depositary shares of Alibaba were down about 4.7%, at $152.25, late Friday afternoon.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: ZHEN DING RESOURCES INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Zhen Ding Resources Inc., a company primarily engaged in the processing of metal ore and selling ore concentrates of gold, silver, lead, zinc, and copper, has released its Form 10-Q report for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in financial performance and outlines key operational developments and future plans.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: The company reported a net income of $429,117 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to a net loss of $460,935 for the same period in 2024. This improvement is primarily due to a gain on the extinguishment of debt.
  • Net Income (Loss) for Three Months Ended September 30, 2025: The net loss for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $(162,828), a decrease of 4.8% compared to the same period in 2024. This reduction is attributed to a modest decrease in general and administrative expenses and interest expenses.
  • Net Income Per Share: The basic and diluted net income per common share for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, was $0.00, reflecting the company's improved financial performance.
  • Operating Loss for Three Months Ended September 30, 2025: The operating loss for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $(41,038), showing a decrease compared to the same period in 2024, indicating effective cost management efforts.
  • Operating Loss for Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025: The operating loss for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, was $(76,499), a decrease compared to the same period in 2024, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Interest Expenses for Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025: Interest expenses were $371,241, showing a slight decrease from the previous year, reflecting reduced financial costs.
  • General and Administrative Expenses for Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025: General and administrative expenses were $76,499, a decrease of 11.2% from the previous year, indicating effective cost control measures.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations are primarily based in China, focusing on the processing of metal ore and selling ore concentrates of gold, silver, lead, zinc, and copper. The joint venture, Zhen Ding JV, is equipped to process ore mined by its partner at the Wuxi Gold Mine.
  • Operational Challenges: The company has faced challenges due to the idling of its mineral processing plant, which was initially shut down in 2017 due to insufficient working capital. This has resulted in no revenue generation during the reporting period.
  • Recent Developments: On December 18, 2024, the Anhui Province Jing County People's Court approved the bankruptcy of the joint venture partner, Xinzhou Gold. Subsequently, the 30% share of the joint venture was transferred to Mr. Wei De Gang, ensuring the continuation of operations under his ownership.
  • Future Outlook: The company is in preliminary discussions with Xinan Environmental Protection (XEP) to develop a waste-to-energy power generation operation using the Wuxi Gold Mine Lands and infrastructure. This collaboration is subject to feasibility study results, negotiation of terms, and government approval.
  • Plan of Operation: The company plans to seek an investment of approximately US$3,350,000 to resume mineral extraction and refinery activities. It is also actively seeking partnerships with mining enterprises to increase its supply of raw material and enhance its capability to access necessary funding.
  • Operational Strategy: The company intends to focus on partnerships with companies in the exploration and extraction of precious and base metals, primarily in China, to provide management expertise and assist in financing efforts. This strategy aims to acquire metal ores for processing and selling ore concentrates to metal refineries.

SEC Filing: ZHEN DING RESOURCES INC. [ RBTK ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: READING INTERNATIONAL INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Reading International Inc., a diversified company primarily engaged in the cinema and real estate sectors, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities during the period, providing insights into its revenue streams, geographical performance, and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $52.17 million for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, reflecting a decrease from $60.09 million in the same period in 2024, primarily due to lower cinema revenue.

Operating Income (Loss): $(329) thousand for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(343) thousand in the same period in 2024, indicating a slight improvement despite lower revenues.

Net Income (Loss): $(4.31) million for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(7.14) million in the same period in 2024, showing a reduction in net loss.

Basic EPS: $(0.18) for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.31) in the same period in 2024, reflecting an improvement in earnings per share.

Diluted EPS: $(0.18) for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.31) in the same period in 2024, consistent with basic EPS.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The cinema segment generated revenue from admissions, concessions, and advertising across the United States, Australia, and New Zealand. The real estate segment earned revenue through property rentals and live theatre operations in the United States, Australia, and New Zealand.

Geographical Performance: In the United States, cinema admissions revenue was $13.8 million for the quarter, with concessions revenue at $9.3 million. In Australia, admissions revenue was $12.4 million, and in New Zealand, it was $1.8 million. The real estate segment in the United States generated $1.95 million in revenue, while Australia contributed $2.39 million.

Sales Units: The cinema segment reported admissions revenue of $40.1 million in the United States, $36.3 million in Australia, and $5.7 million in New Zealand for the nine months ended September 30, 2025.

Future Outlook: Management anticipates improvements in cinema patronage and operating revenue levels with an increase in the quantity and quality of films being released. The company plans to raise liquidity through real estate asset monetizations and refinancing efforts to address going concern uncertainties.

SEC Filing: READING INTERNATIONAL INC [ RDI ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: MacKenzie Realty Capital, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
MacKenzie Realty Capital, Inc., a company specializing in leasing office space and apartment units, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, highlighting significant improvements in financial performance and operational developments. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial health and business activities for the recent quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $4.54 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year's $4.95 million, primarily due to lower rental income.
  • Operating Loss: $(3.49) million, improved from the previous year's $(7.44) million, indicating better cost management and reduced impairment losses.
  • Net Income: $(3.05) million, a significant improvement from the previous year's $(7.41) million, driven by reduced operating expenses and unrealized gains on equity securities.
  • Net Loss Attributable to Common Stockholders: $(4.09) million, compared to $(8.14) million in the prior year, showing a narrowing of losses.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Share: $(2.20), improved from $(6.10) in the previous year, reflecting the impact of the reverse stock split and reduced net loss.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company generates rental revenue by leasing office space and apartment units to tenants. These leases are classified as operating leases, and revenues are recognized on a straight-line basis over the lease terms.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates several properties across different locations, including Oakland, CA; Hollywood, CA; Concord, CA; Duluth, GA; Napa, CA; Woodland, CA; Fairfield, CA; and Suisun, CA. Each location has varying numbers of tenants and property types, contributing to the overall operational performance.
  • Sales Units: The company manages multiple properties with a total of 14 tenants at the Green Valley Medical Center and 14 tenants at Aurora at Green Valley, indicating a stable occupancy rate across its portfolio.
  • New Production Launches: The company completed the construction of Aurora at Green Valley, which consists of three residential buildings and a clubhouse. The clubhouse opened in June 2025, and the first residential building was completed in July 2025, with leasing commencing in August 2025. The remaining two buildings were completed in August and September 2025.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to commence construction of the Blue Ridge at Suisun Valley in spring 2026, subject to city approval and securing necessary financial resources. This development is expected to enhance the company's portfolio and operational capacity.

SEC Filing: MacKenzie Realty Capital, Inc. [ MKZR ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Lightstone Value Plus REIT IV, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Lightstone Value Plus REIT IV, Inc., a real estate investment trust focused on real estate and real estate-related investments, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities for the period ended September 30, 2025.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $8.2 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $8.5 million for the same period in 2024. The decrease was primarily due to a reduction in food, beverage, and other revenues.

Net Loss: $(374) thousand for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(1,369) thousand for the same period in 2024. The improvement was driven by a decrease in interest expense and a net casualty gain.

Net Loss Per Company’s Common Shares, Basic and Diluted: $(0.05) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.14) for the same period in 2024.

Total Revenue: $21.9 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $21.3 million for the same period in 2024. The increase was due to higher room revenues reflecting improved occupancy and ADR.

Net Loss: $(3,979) thousand for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(8,165) thousand for the same period in 2024. The reduction in net loss was attributed to lower interest expenses and a net casualty gain.

Net Loss Per Company’s Common Shares, Basic and Diluted: $(0.39) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.79) for the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

[Hotel Operations]: The Williamsburg Moxy Hotel, a 216-room Marriott branded hotel located in Brooklyn, New York, experienced a high occupancy rate of 91% for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. The hotel reported a RevPAR of $244.77 and an ADR of $270.11, indicating strong performance in room revenue.

[Casualty Event and Recovery]: The Williamsburg Moxy Hotel suffered damage from a fire in December 2024, affecting one of its food and beverage venues. The hotel has filed an insurance claim and received advances totaling $1.5 million, with expectations of further recoveries related to business interruption.

[Joint Venture Developments]: The Williamsburg Moxy Hotel Joint Venture, in which the company holds a 75% interest, continues to be a significant part of operations. Lightstone REIT III holds the remaining 25% interest, and the joint venture is managed by the company.

[Real Estate Development]: The 40 East End Ave. Joint Venture, in which the company holds a 33.3% interest, has sold 27 out of 29 units in its luxury residential condominium project in Manhattan, New York. The remaining two units are expected to contribute to future cash flows.

[Future Outlook]: The company anticipates that cash flows from the Williamsburg Moxy Hotel and potential sales from the 40 East End Ave. Joint Venture will be sufficient to meet operational and financial obligations over the next 12 months. The company is also considering additional capital contributions from Lightstone REIT III if necessary.

[Operational Strategy]: The company continues to focus on real estate and real estate-related investments, including operating properties and development projects. The strategy includes potential investments in mezzanine loans, mortgage loans, and preferred equity interests, with a focus on development-related opportunities.

SEC Filing: Lightstone Value Plus REIT IV, Inc. [ LTSV ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: GOOD GAMING, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Good Gaming, Inc., a company previously known for its involvement in Minecraft server management and esports tournaments, has released its Q3 2023 10-Q report. The report highlights significant changes in the company's business strategy and provides a detailed overview of its financial performance during the reporting period. The company is now focusing on the mobile gaming market through a new partnership with ViaOne Services, marking a strategic pivot from its previous operations.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: $0, reflecting a decrease of $174 or 100% compared to the same period in 2024 due to the decision to halt Minecraft development.

Gross Loss: $(18,689), indicating a reduction in revenue-generating activities.

Operating Loss: $(49,669), a decrease of $140,853 or 73.91% from the previous year, attributed to reduced operating expenses.

Net Loss: $(50,675), a decrease of $127,594 or 71.57% from the previous year, primarily due to a decrease in operating expenses.

Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Common Share: $(0.00), consistent with the previous year, reflecting the company's ongoing financial challenges.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company has halted its Minecraft development, which contributed to a decrease in revenue for the reporting period.

New Product Launches: The company is focusing on pre-installing games on mobile devices through a partnership with ViaOne Services. This represents a shift in business strategy towards mobile gaming distribution.

Operational Strategy: The company has exited its previous business operations, including managing Minecraft servers and hosting esports tournaments, to focus on distributing mobile games by pre-installing them on devices sold by mobile phone service providers.

Future Outlook: The company plans to create partnerships with game developers to pre-install games on thousands of devices, measure the results through controlled tests, and seek additional game developers and publishers for player acquisition.

Operational Changes: The company has ceased operations on certain projects that were not profitable, leading to a reduction in operating expenses and a focus on new strategic initiatives.

Business Focus: The company is at an early stage of its new business operations, emphasizing the need for additional capital to fund its activities and achieve its operational milestones.

SEC Filing: GOOD GAMING, INC. [ GMER ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: BrooQLy Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
BrooQLy Inc., now operating as Dynamic Aerospace Systems Corporation, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025. The report highlights the company's strategic shift towards UAV manufacturing and autonomous logistics, following significant acquisitions and rebranding efforts. This transition marks a pivotal moment for the company as it aims to establish itself in the aerospace and logistics sectors.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $0 - The company generated no revenue for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting its transition to a new business model focused on UAV manufacturing and autonomous logistics.
  • Loss from operations: $(1.1) million - The loss from operations for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was primarily due to increased professional fees and general and administrative costs associated with the acquisition and integration of new business assets.
  • Net loss: $(1.3) million - The net loss for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was driven by operating expenses and interest and other expenses, reflecting the company's investment in scaling operations and preparing for future growth.
  • Net Loss Per Common Stock - basic and fully diluted: $(0.04) - The net loss per share increased due to higher operating expenses and interest costs associated with the company's strategic acquisitions and business expansion.

Business Highlights

  • Segment Operations: BrooQLy Inc., now operating as Dynamic Aerospace Systems Corporation, has transitioned its focus to two main segments: Dynamic Aerospace Systems (DAS) and Dynamic Deliveries. DAS is involved in the manufacturing of UAVs, while Dynamic Deliveries focuses on autonomous mesh logistics networks.
  • Business Transition: The company rebranded to Dynamic Aerospace Systems to align with its strategic focus on UAV innovation and logistics, following the acquisition of assets from Vayu (US) Inc., Impossible Aerospace Corporation, and Global Autonomous Corporation.
  • Operational Focus: The company is concentrating on developing and commercializing advanced VTOL drones and UAV systems, such as the G1 and US-1, for applications in autonomous logistics, surveillance, and reconnaissance.
  • Geographical Performance: The company serves government, defense, and commercial sectors across the United States, Gulf Coast nations, and NATO countries, focusing on optimizing efficiency and reducing risk through autonomous aerial solutions.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates that commercial activity from acquired assets will begin offsetting operating costs over the coming quarters. The company is also planning to expand its manufacturing capabilities and pursue additional contracts with government and commercial clients.
  • Strategic Investments: Significant investments have been made in R&D, manufacturing, and operational costs, particularly in enhancing proprietary UAV designs with autonomous flight controls and advanced sensor integration.
  • Board Expertise: The appointment of Jorge L. Torres, a FedEx logistics expert, to the Board of Directors is expected to enhance the company's strategic expertise in logistics and supply chain optimization.
  • Future Developments: The company plans to conduct flight testing at Strother Field, Kansas, expected to be operational in 2026, and is exploring further equity and debt financing to support ongoing R&D and expansion efforts.

SEC Filing: BrooQLy Inc. [ BRQL ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025
